sections.purpose: White content band: eyebrow + h2 + two body paragraphs (no image).

props.title: Elevate Your Practice — Literally
paragraphs: Master the art of yoga in the air with our Aerial Yoga Teacher Training Course at Bodhi School of Yoga. Build strength, grace, and confidence while learning how to teach this unique and therapeutic style of yoga using hammock.

You'll combine traditional yoga postures with graceful aerial movements — learning how inversions decompress the spine, build core stability, and create a deeply healing experience for your future students.

components.notes: Two-column layout in Figma: left = eyebrow+h2, right = body paragraphs. Eyebrow color #009877 → text-text-brand.

sections.purpose: What You'll Gain — eyebrow + h2 + 6-card grid (2 cols x 3 rows), each card = icon tile + title + body.

items.title: Yoga Meets Flight
items.body: Combine traditional poses with graceful aerial movements.

items.title: Relieve & Realign
items.body: Inversions decompress the spine and boost flexibility.

items.title: Build Strength
items.body: Improve core stability, balance, and control.

items.title: Therapeutic & Fun
items.body: Enjoy emotional release through playful, healing practice.

items.title: Teach with an Edge
items.body: Gain a unique certification and grow your yoga career with a skill few teachers have.

items.title: Join a Community
items.body: Connect with passionate practitioners and expert teachers at Bodhi School of Yoga.

components.notes: Eyebrow color #005564 → may need text-text-tertiary or new token. Icon tiles are 52x52 rounded squares with #e4fff9 background.

sections.purpose: FAQ section — eyebrow + h2 + 4-item accordion (question + '+' toggle, first item shown expanded with answer body).

items.question: Do I need prior experience in aerial yoga?
items.answer: No prior aerial yoga experience is required. A basic understanding of yoga postures and physical fitness to perform inversions is sufficient to join this course.

items.question: Is this TTC Yoga Alliance certified?
Will I get to practice on hammocks during training?
Can this course help me start my own aerial yoga classes?

components.notes: Only the first item shows answer copy in Figma; the other three answers are not in the design (use placeholder or pull from CMS). Accordion uses horizontal borders + a '+' toggle that should rotate to '×' or '−' on open.
Repeated 4x (count: 4). Use a Radix/headless accordion primitive if one exists in the codebase.

uncertain.reason: Rectangle 161124039 (2624x989, x=-295, y=-10) is a bleed background behind the hero. Folded into hero section as decorative bg — not a standalone component.
Rectangle 161124050 (328x306, x=1562, y=2869) is the green decorative panel behind the instructor scroller arrow. Folded into instructors section.

Group 1171281776 contains an extra 'Business of Aerial Yoga' card (1:7763) at x=1966, y=2087 sitting beyond the viewport — confirms the syllabus row is a horizontal scroller, but the same card content also exists inside 1:8029. Treat as a single item in props, not a duplicate.
Each pre-req checklist item appears twice in Figma (likely default + hover state mirrored vertically). Collapsed to 3 unique items in EligibilitySection.
SectionTabNav has spelling 'Circulum' (should be Curriculum) and 'Overall' (likely 'Reviews' or 'Overview' / typo). Preserved verbatim — designer should confirm intent.

Only `imgMaskGroup` is unambiguously the first instructor's photo. Other three avatar image_refs assigned by ordinal guess (imgRectangle161124054, 055, 056) — codebase-scout / component-builder should verify against the design-context payload.
Only the first FAQ has visible answer body in Figma. Remaining three need placeholder copy or CMS content.
